Text: I enjoyed the food itself, the chef is a wonder! We chose a menu for 1500 per person, ordered a dish from the head chef and another salad, but even without our extras, there would have been enough treats.

The pricing at this establishment is reasonable: a bowl of solyanka soup costs 200 RUB, Nemiroff vodka is 80 RUB/800 RUB, a glass of wine (I can't remember which one) is 150 RUB, Kent cigarettes with a lighter are 100 RUB, and some kind of draught Ukrainian beer (tasty though) is 150 RUB (bottled is 130 RUB). The total bill for three people, including alcohol, came to 1470 RUB plus tea.
We were satisfied with the food every time - the salads were always made from fresh vegetables, incredibly tasty khachapuri, and a very respectable lagman.
The staff turned out to be very attentive and polite, starting from the lady hostess downstairs, ending with several waiters.
Just like 3 years ago, there's nothing new on the menu, just pizzas and pastas.
In my opinion, new dishes should be added to the menu at least once a year so that people find it interesting to try something new.
Very attentive waiters and administrators.

The only thing that was slightly unpleasant was that we waited a long time for service to start (probably due to the large number of people), and no one cleared the dishes from neighboring tables when the people left.
Thank you for the "Tower" steak first of all to the chef, it was very well cooked, and secondly to the girl for her advice.
The portion was very generous!
Although the couches could have been made cozier, more "homely", with cushions.
The interior is nothing special, clean and quiet, music in the adjacent hall, but we specifically wanted it without music.
The food was enjoyable, especially the dessert - Napoleon, very tasty).
Administrator Victoria immediately drew up the menu, took into account all our wishes, and gave advice on conducting the banquet.
We waited for a very long time because the girl (see above) was dressing and undressing the guests.
The food is always tasty.
the black caesar was also very decent, made with taste.
The service is excellent, the waitresses did a great job helping us pack not only the food but also all the flowers that were given to us after the party!
We will recommend the restaurant to our acquaintances.

The Caesar salad consisted of the following components: at the bottom were four large pieces cut from a baguette, on top were lettuce leaves, then some tomatoes and four large strips of chicken, there was no parmesan at all, cool?
The menu really made me spend a long time choosing, and not because there was nothing to choose from, but rather because I wanted to try so many things.
Excellent cuisine.
Pleasant interior - very light on the soul and cozy.

It's a pity there's no lamb shish kebab, but they just opened, so I think the menu will expand! I tried a piece of pork from my husband's portion—it was tender and juicy; I haven't eaten anything like it in ages, unlike the usual overcooked stuff)))) The Caesar salad was the tastiest version I've ever had (I've tried Caesar salads in so many places! ), and the kvass was very tasty too. Everything we ordered was to our liking, and both my husband and I are extremely picky eaters. Well done.
For the hotel restaurant, I think it's too dark and gloomy inside, but it's nice outside with wooden tables and chairs under umbrellas, fresh air - that's where we settled.
As it was many years ago, the service is at a high level.
And what's very nice - they bring a plate with water and lemon for washing hands when eating shashlik: I love to eat shashlik with my hands.
Great restaurant!
All my guests were very satisfied, by the way, everyone who ate the steak remembers it :)
I will tell a wonderful story about how at the restaurant "Troika" they value their customers.
Of course, I can't help but mention the beer! For it alone, I would create a scale and give it an 11 out of 10! That is, better than excellent!
And they greet, and see off (without scary screams in Japanese:)), and repeat orders, and maintain the sequence of dishes, and change ashtrays without an additional request.
The food was brought very quickly, the waiter answered any question about the menu without hesitation, he worked very professionally.

Regarding the kitchen: we really enjoyed the cod with spinach, it was the most delicate cod we've ever had, and the roasted vegetable salad impressed us as well. Overall, everything pleased us, we left satisfied and full.
The food is generally tasty, no complaints, portion sizes are reasonable.
The interior is pleasant, the lighting is soft, the seats are comfortable, the music is well-chosen)
A small cozy cafe.
Service was enjoyable, staff is polite, orders are brought fairly quickly.
